decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly lower today with NASDAQ down 0.5% and S&P 500 down 0.2%, signaling a cautious but not alarming session. Cash sits at $477 — just under the $500 minimum needed to open new positions — so despite strong momentum candidates like FND, TXG, and LPX all scoring 95, no new buys are made. Current six holdings are maintained; no stop-loss or profit target triggers are visible from today's data.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities edged lower on June 24, 2026, with the NASDAQ underperforming as tech sentiment remained subdued in the absence of a strong directional catalyst. The S&P 500's modest -0.2% decline reflects a market in consolidation mode rather than a trend reversal, with investors cautious ahead of upcoming economic data. Summer trading patterns — characterized by lower volume and reduced conviction — are contributing to the choppy, range-bound price action seen this week.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月24日，美國股市小幅收低，那斯達克指數在缺乏明確催化劑的情況下表現落後大市，科技板塊情緒偏謹慎。標普500僅跌0.2%，顯示市場處於整固階段而非趨勢逆轉，投資者在等待下一輪經濟數據前態度保守。夏季交投量偏低、市場方向感不足的季節性因素，進一步加劇了本週震盪橫行的走勢。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag is the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio — cash earns little or nothing while markets move, pulling down your overall returns compared to being fully deployed. Today's portfolio has $477 sitting idle, just below the $500 threshold to open a new position, which means four open slots go unfilled despite high-conviction candidates being available. Over time, even small cash balances can meaningfully reduce compounded returns.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合持有未投資現金所帶來的績效損失——現金幾乎沒有回報，而市場卻持續運作，導致整體收益率落後於滿倉操作的策略。今日投資組合閒置477美元現金，略低於500美元的入市門檻，儘管有多隻高分動能股可供選擇，仍有四個持倉空位未能填補。長期而言，即使是小額閒置現金，也會對複利回報造成明顯侵蝕。
